name: Between pines, lakes and ocean — Les Landes
name_original: Entre pins, lacs et océan — Les Landes
description:
This route offers you a beautiful immersion in the Landes forest while discovering magnificent lakes and villages. The departure is located in the parking lot of the Isle Verte sports center in the village of Soustons. The route begins on a small footbridge which spans part of Lake Soustons.

You then cross part of the town before quickly finding yourself immersed in the imposing Landes de Gascogne forest. The largest forest in Europe, it offers you a peaceful and pleasant setting. Like much of the route, this portion has very little elevation change. This leads to the village of Vieux-Boucau. You then enter the seaside resort along its superb marine lake.

Its developed banks, beaches and water activities contrast with the preserved island in its middle. Reserved for birds, this site and the effects of the tides guarantee a magnificent natural spectacle. Vieux-Boucau is also an ideal place for a refreshing swim or a gourmet break.

A new flat and pleasant section follows which leads to Messanges, a small village nestled under the pines not far from the ocean. You continue on a slightly hilly section towards Azur. This is where you find Lake Soustons. Take the time to visit its emblematic pontoon before returning to Soustons along the eastern shore of the lake. Along the way, take a detour to the bucolic Pointe des Vergnes and its century-old trees inhabited by numerous squirrels.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Soustons is characterized by its flat terrain, extensive network of cycle paths, and diverse natural landscapes. The region features numerous saltwater and freshwater lakes, the vast Landes Forest, and Atlantic ocean beaches with dunes. These physical features combine to create a varied environment suitable for touring cyclists of all abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Soustons The most popular touring cycling route is Between pines, lakes and ocean — Les Landes, a 17.6 miles (28.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete, winding through pine forests and past coastal areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Soustons Pond – former train station loop from Camping Sandaya Soustons Village, an easy 19.8 miles (31.8 km) path. This route circles the Soustons Pond, offering views of the water and surrounding natural areas. Local touring cyclists also love the Vieux-Boucau Marine Lake – Soustons Stream loop from Soustons, a 15.3 miles (24.6 km) trail leading through areas around the Marine Lake and Soustons Stream, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Touring cycling around Soustons is defined by its numerous lakes, the extensive Landes Forest, and coastal paths along the Atlantic. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, flat rides to longer excursions. The routes in Soustons are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 750 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Soustons's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Soustons?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Soustons, catering to various skill levels. A significant majority, 38 routes, are classified as easy, making the region very accessible for all cyclists.

Q: What is the terrain like for touring cycling in Soustons?
A: The terrain around Soustons is predominantly flat, making it ideal for touring cyclists of all abilities. The region boasts an extensive network of paved cycle paths, often separate from roads, ensuring a safe and pleasant experience through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families with children?
A: Yes, Soustons is very family-friendly for cycling. The relatively flat terrain and numerous dedicated cycle paths make it accessible for beginners and families. Many routes are easy, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41049815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Étang de Soustons – Soustons Pond loop from Soustons&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 13 km and offers gentle riding around the lake.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Soustons?
A: Cyclists in Soustons can enjoy a rich variety of landscapes. Routes often traverse the vast Landes Forest with its shaded pine trails, wind around the region's five distinct lakes (including Soustons Lake and the Marine Lake of Port d'Albret), and extend along the Atlantic coast, offering views of ocean beaches and dunes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Soustons?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Soustons are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24851749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soustons Pond – former train station loop from Camping Sandaya Soustons Village&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the Soustons Pond, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39291823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vieux-Boucau Marine Lake – Soustons Stream loop from Soustons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover along the cycling paths?
A: The cycling paths in Soustons offer access to numerous natural attractions. You can explore the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3467910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;West Lake of Soustons&lt;/a&gt;, discover the preserved ecosystems of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6068030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang Noir Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, or cycle through the extensive Landes Forest. The region's lakes, ocean beaches, and dunes provide stunning natural scenery.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or points of interest to visit while cycling?
A: Yes, several points of interest can be found along the routes. You might encounter artistic sculptures on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3542414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélodyssée Cycle Path between Soustons and Vieux-Boucau-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the vibrant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6988445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capbreton Port and Town Center&lt;/a&gt; if your route extends south. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vélodyssée via Messanges&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic sections.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with panoramic views on the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region offers several spots for panoramic views. The Pointe des Vergnes, a peninsula within Soustons Lake, provides exceptional vistas, especially from its observatory. Additionally, routes along the coast or through elevated sections of the Landes Forest can offer beautiful perspectives of the diverse landscape, including the ocean and dunes.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Soustons?
A: The touring cycling routes around Soustons are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 750 reviews. Cyclists often praise the well-maintained, flat paths, the variety of landscapes from lakes to forests and the ocean, and the overall accessibility for different fitness levels.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Soustons?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Soustons is generally from spring through early autumn (April to October). During these months, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summer offers warm temperatures perfect for combining cycling with beach activities, while spring and autumn provide cooler weather and beautiful natural colors.

Q: Are the cycling paths in Soustons separate from car traffic?
A: Many of the cycling paths in Soustons, especially those integrated into major European routes like the Vélodyssée and the Scandibérique, are separate from roads. This ensures a high level of safety and a more relaxed cycling experience, particularly when exploring the forests, lakes, and coastal areas.
tips: Magnificent portion which connects Soustons to the seaside resort of Vieux-Boucau and its beautiful marine lake. You move in the shade of the pine forest.

The Vieux-Boucau marine lake is located very close to the ocean as well as the town center of the seaside resort. Its beaches and water activities contrast with the island reserved for birds in its middle.
At the very north of Lake Soustons, on the banks of the commune of Azur, is this superb pontoon which offers a magnificent view of the lake and the forest which surrounds it.

Superb wooded park located on the banks of the Soustons pond where bald cypresses or Louisiana cypresses reign supreme. Not very shy, countless squirrels have taken up residence in this beautiful park, a spectacle not to be missed. The Batby Inn located at the entrance to the park is an excellent restaurant.

waypoints.name: Artistic Sculptures on the Vélodyssée Cycle Path (Soustons – Vieux-Boucau-les-Bains)
Bassin and Awron Bernard Bourandy Tower, Étang de Soustons
